name: Moleculin Biotech, Inc.
business: Moleculin Biotech, Inc. is a late-stage pharmaceutical development company evaluating Annamycin, a next-generation anthracycline, for the treatment of relapsed/refractory acute myeloid leukemia (AML) and other cancers. The company also has two additional portfolios of technologies for hard-to-treat cancers and viruses, including the WP1066 immune/transcription modulator portfolio and the WP1122 glycolysis inhibitor portfolio.
sellsTo: patients with soft tissue sarcoma lung metastases
patients with other hard-to-treat cancers and viruses

exposures.sentence: Annamycin is what we believe to be a next-generation anthracycline (one of the most widely used classes of chemotherapy), designed to be different than currently approved anthracyclines, which are limited in utility because of cardiotoxicity risks, tolerability, their susceptibility to multidrug resistance mechanisms and a lack of efficacy in certain tumor models.

Our WP1066 Portfolio includes WP1066, WP1193 and WP1220, three of several Immune/Transcription Modulators in the portfolio designed to inhibit p-STAT3 (phosphorylated signal transducer and activator of transcription) among other transcription factors associated with tumor activity.
Our WP1122 Portfolio contains compounds (including WP1122, WP1096, and WP1097) designed to exploit the potential uses of inhibitors of glycolysis such as 2-deoxy-D-glucose (2-DG).

catalysts.event: Interim unblinding of data for first 45 subjects treated in Part A of MIRACLE trial
catalysts.sentence: we expect to have an interim unblinding of data in mid-2026 and an additional unblinding in the second half of 2026, thereby concluding Part A of the two-part trial.

catalysts.event: Additional unblinding of data for up to 90 subjects in Part A of MIRACLE trial
catalysts.sentence: we expect to have an interim unblinding of data in mid-2026 and an additional unblinding in the second half of 2026, thereby concluding Part A of the two-part trial.

catalysts.event: Publication of clinical study report (CSR) for MB-106 trial
catalysts.sentence: MB-106, which was a Phase 1B/2 trial treating AML with AnnAraC has achieved data lock, and the clinical study report (CSR) is in process and should be published in late March 2026.

catalysts.event: Filing of resale registration statement for Series H warrants
catalysts.sentence: We have agreed to file a resale registration statement covering the shares of common stock issuable upon exercise of the Series H warrants on or before March 31, 2026, and to use commercially reasonable efforts to cause such registration statement to be declared effective by April 30, 2026.

catalysts.event: Effectiveness of resale registration statement for Series H warrants
catalysts.sentence: We have agreed to file a resale registration statement covering the shares of common stock issuable upon exercise of the Series H warrants on or before March 31, 2026, and to use commercially reasonable efforts to cause such registration statement to be declared effective by April 30, 2026.

catalysts.event: Start of Phase 1B/2 clinical trial for Annamycin as monotherapy treating 3rd line pancreatic cancer
catalysts.sentence: Externally funded Based on sponsored research, we intend to begin in 2026 a Phase 1B/2 clinical trial with Annamycin as a monotherapy treating 3rd line pancreatic cancer.

catalysts.event: Planned start of MB-109 (AnnAraC for 3rd line R/R AML in adults)
catalysts.sentence: Internally funded MB-109 utilizing AnnAraC for R/R AML as 3rd line therapy in adults is planned for 2027.

catalysts.event: Planned start of MB-110 (AnnAraC for 2nd line R/R AML in pediatrics)
catalysts.sentence: Externally funded MB-110 utilizing AnnAraC for R/R AML as 2nd line therapy in pediatrics is planned for 2027.

tamClaims.claim: There are approximately 150,000 people with AML worldwide with over 20,000 newly diagnosed patients annually in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: There are approximately 150,000 people with AML worldwide with over 20,000 newly diagnosed patients annually in the U.S. (https://www.cancer.org/cancer/types/acute-myeloid-leukemia/about/key-statistics.html).
